title: Systems and methods for determining the location and orientation of implanted devices
abstract: The present disclosure relates to computerized systems and methods for determining the location and orientation of implanted devices. Consistent with some embodiments, power levels of near-field signals are backscattered from an implanted device. The backscattered near-field signals may be detected by an ex-vivo antenna array. The determined power levels may be used to identify candidate location and orientation combinations based on a data set. The data set may be generated by calibrating the antenna array to the implanted device. A candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations may be selected as the location and orientation of the implanted device.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A system for determining the location and orientation of an implanted device, the system comprising: a memory device that stores a set of instructions; and at least one processor that executes the instructions: determine power levels of near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device, the near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device being detected by an ex-vivo antenna array; identify candidate location and orientation combinations corresponding to the determined power levels based on a data set; and select a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 2. The system according to claim 1 , wherein the data set is included in at least one of a look-up table, an XML tree, and a spreadsheet. 3. The system according to claim 1 , wherein the at least one processor executes the instructions to select a candidate location and orientation combination by: determining, among the identified candidate combinations, a location and orientation combination that corresponds to a plurality of the determined power levels; and selecting the determined location and orientation combination as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 4. The system according to claim 1 , wherein the antenna array is configured to: transmit near-field signals to the implanted device; and detect near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device. 5. The system according to claim 4, wherein the at least one processor further executes the instructions to: determine power levels of near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device and detected at each element in the antenna array; and identify candidate location and orientation combinations included in a plurality of data sets that correspond to the determined power levels, each of the plurality of data sets associated with specific ones of the elements in the antenna array. 6. The system according to claim 5, wherein the at least one processor further executes the instructions to: determine, among the identified candidate combinations, a location and orientation combination identified in each of the plurality of look-up tables; and select the determined location and orientation combination as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 7. The system according to claim 4, wherein the near-field signals transmitted to the implanted device by the antenna array power the implanted device wirelessly. 8. The system according to claim 1 , wherein the at least one processor executes the instructions to select the location and orientation of the implanted device by: generating a model of power levels included in the data set; iterating through the identified candidate combinations using the model based on optimization algorithm; and selecting a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations as the location and orientation of the implanted device based on the iteration through the identified candidate combinations. 9. The system according to claim 8, wherein the at least one processor executes the instructions to select the location and orientation of the implanted device by: selecting a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations that produces the lowest error of the optimization algorithm as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 10. A method for determining a location and orientation of an implanted device, the method performed by at least one processor and comprising: determining power levels of near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device, the near-field signals being detected by an ex-vivo antenna array; identifying candidate location and orientation combinations corresponding to the determined power levels based on a data set; and selecting a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 1 1 . The method according to claim 10, wherein the data set is included in at least one of a look-up table, an XML tree, and a spreadsheet. 12. The method according to claim 10, wherein selecting a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations includes: determining, among the identified candidate combinations, a location and orientation combination that corresponds to a plurality of the determined power levels; and selecting the determined location and orientation combination as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 13. The method according to claim 10, further comprising: transmitting, with the antenna, near-field signals to the implanted device; and detecting, with the antenna, near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein: determining power levels of near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device includes determining power levels of near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device detected at each element in the antenna array; and identifying candidate location and orientation combinations includes identifying candidate location and orientation combinations included in a plurality of data sets that correspond to the determined power levels, each of the plurality of data sets associated with specific ones of the elements in the antenna array. 15. The method according to claim 14, further comprising: determining, among the identified candidate combinations, a location and orientation combination identified in each of the plurality of look-up tables; and selecting the determined location and orientation combination as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 16. The method according to claim 13, wherein the near-field signals transmitted to the implanted device by the antenna array power the implanted device wirelessly. 17. The method according to claim 10, wherein selecting the candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations includes: generating a model of power levels included in the data set; iterating through the identified candidate combinations using the model based on optimization algorithm; and selecting a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations as the location and orientation of the implanted device based on the iteration through the identified candidate combinations. 18. The method according to claim 17, wherein selecting the candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations includes: selecting a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations that produces the lowest error of the optimization algorithm as the location and orientation of the implanted device. 19. A non-transitory computer-readable medium including instructions that, when executed by at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to: determine power levels of near-field signals backscattered from an implanted device, the near-field signals backscattered from the implanted device being detected by an ex-vivo antenna array; identify candidate location and orientation combinations corresponding to the determined power levels based on a data set; and 

 select a candidate location and orientation combination among the identified candidate combinations as the location and orientation of the implanted device.
